title.es: El Problema de la Induccion en Finanzas
title.en: The Problem of Induction in Finance

summary_es: Este modulo aborda el problema filosofico mas profundo de la modelacion financiera: la induccion. Usando la metafora del pavo de Russell, se demuestra que la evidencia historica acumulada no garantiza resultados futuros. Se conecta el falsacionismo de Popper con la practica financiera: buscar refutar estrategias en lugar de confirmarlas. Se compara como los enfoques frecuentista y bayesiano manejan la fragilidad inductiva, mostrando que el bayesiano acota su confianza mediante priors mientras el frecuentista la expande sin limite. Se implementan herramientas practicas: deteccion de cambio de regimen con ventana deslizante, stress testing inductivo para cuantificar perdidas bajo escenarios de falla, y tests falsacionistas para evaluar la robustez de estrategias de trading.
summary_en: This module addresses the deepest philosophical problem in financial modeling: induction. Using Russell's turkey metaphor, it demonstrates that accumulated historical evidence does not guarantee future results. Popper's falsificationism is connected to financial practice: seeking to refute strategies rather than confirm them. Frequentist and Bayesian approaches to inductive fragility are compared, showing that Bayesian bounds its confidence through priors while frequentist expands it without limit. Practical tools are implemented: regime change detection with sliding windows, inductive stress testing to quantify losses under failure scenarios, and falsificationist tests to evaluate trading strategy robustness.
learning_objectives_es: Entender el problema de la induccion de Hume y su impacto en modelos financieros
Simular el pavo de Russell con datos financieros sinteticos
Comparar fragilidad inductiva de modelos frecuentistas vs bayesianos
Implementar deteccion de cambio de regimen con ventana deslizante
Aplicar stress testing inductivo para cuantificar riesgo de falla
learning_objectives_en: Understand Hume's problem of induction and its impact on financial models
Simulate Russell's turkey with synthetic financial data
Compare inductive fragility of frequentist vs Bayesian models
Implement regime change detection with sliding windows
Apply inductive stress testing to quantify failure risk

parameters.name: Uncertainty Quantification
parameters.origin_phrase: Induction cannot prove future outcomes; only quantify uncertainty about them

parameters.name: Non-Stationarity
parameters.origin_phrase: Financial processes change structurally, breaking all inductive assumptions

parameters.name: Non-Probabilistic Risk Flaws
parameters.origin_phrase: Frequentist confidence grows without bound, masking inductive fragility

parameters.name: Model Robustness
parameters.origin_phrase: Stress testing quantifies what induction ignores

parameters.name: Knowledge Integration
parameters.origin_phrase: Skeptical priors encode awareness that past patterns may break
